data.title: Dividend Roundup: Disney, Microsoft, Dell Technologies, Alphabet, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Disney (NYSE:DIS) and Deere (DE) as well as declarations from companies such as Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) and FedEx (FDX) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Aflac (AFL)

  • Dividend declared: December 2
  • New dividend amount: $0.58
  • Previous amount: $0.50
  • Increase: 16%

Deere (DE)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • New dividend amount: $1.62
  • Previous amount: $1.47
  • Increase: 10.2%

Disney (DIS)

  • Dividend declared: December 4
  • New dividend amount: $0.50
  • Previous amount: $0.45
  • Increase: 11.1%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Microsoft (MSFT)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • Dividend amount: $0.93

Dell Technologies (DELL)

  • Dividend declared: December 3
  • Dividend amount: $0.445

Verizon (VZ)

  • Dividend declared: December 5
  • Dividend amount: $0.6775

Salesforce (CRM)

  • Dividend declared: December 5
  • Dividend amount: $0.40

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 9
  • Payout date: December 16

FedEx (FDX)

  • Ex-dividend date: November 26
  • Payout date: December 26

Ross Stores (ROST)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 10
  • Payout date: December 31

Devon Energy (DVN)

  • Ex-dividend date: December 13
  • Payout date: December 31

data.title: Stellantis CFO gives assurances company is moving in the right direction
data.description:
Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) shares are getting a lift Friday morning thanks to encouraging remarks from company executives at a roadshow organized by analysts at French banking firm, Oddo BHF.

According to media reports, Stellantis (STLA) CFO Doug Ostermann assured analysts that the company is equipped to handle the challenges it faces and is confident in the company’s performance in 2025 and beyond.

“The CFO expressed confidence in the automaker's ability to rebound and improve its performance next year, with, in our opinion, a seasonality in favor of [the second half] partly linked to launches,” said Oddo BHF analyst Michael Foundoukidis, adding that the changes at the head of Stellantis should enable the process of overhaul to be accelerated and intensified in the coming weeks, pending the appointment of the future CEO.

During a visit to the Maserati factory in Modena, Italy, Ostermann told employees the company is working to rebuild trust with dealers, suppliers, unions, and government officials following a “period of tension” under former CEO Carlos Tavares, according to Bloomberg.

Stellantis (STLA) shares are up 4% in premarket trading and set to extend a three-day winning streak to a fourth day.

data.title: BofA upgrades Molson Coors, expects U.S. beer industry volumes to improve in 2025
data.description:
BofA on Friday upgraded Molson Coors Beverage (NYSE:TAP) to Buy from Neutral and raised its price objective from $57 to $70, expecting 2025 to be a 'more normal year' given that the beer industry has fully digested the Bud Light controversy.

Shares of Molson Coors (TAP) rose +2.53% to $62.50 in premarket trade, having shed about 1.7% in value in the past one year.

The U.S. beer industry should perform more in line with historical levels after a challenging 2024, BofA said, adding that TAP faces easy year-ago comparisons and no headwind related to the termination of the Pabst contract brewing agreement.

"We expect cost inflation to be manageable despite the prospect for higher aluminum cost," the investment bank said.

It also expects strong free cash flow and modest debt leverage to enable TAP to continue to repurchase shares, which is reflected in BofA's above-consensus FY25 EPS estimate of $6.0 vs consensus estimate of $5.96.

Overall, if the market strength post election continues into next year and there is a melt-up in value stocks, BofA analysts view Molson Coors (TAP) as the most attractive option in its consumer staples coverage universe.

BofA's forecast assumes that TAP’s share loss improves to a 30bps decline from a 110bps decline.

Seeking Alpha analysts have a Buy rating on the company.

The top-selling beers in the U.S. include Constellation Brands' (STZ) Modelo Especial and Molson Coors' (TAP) Coors Light.

data.title: BRP to buyback up to 3.31M subordinate voting shares
data.description:
• Toronto Stock Exchange has approved BRP (DOOO:CA) renewal of its normal course issuer bid to purchase for cancellation up to 3,331,852 subordinate voting shares (10% of public float) over the twelve-month period ending no later than December 9, 2025.
  • Under the last NCIB that expired on December 4, 2024, the company purchased 3,231,999 subordinate voting shares.
  • The board believes that the purchase by BRP of its subordinate voting shares could represent an appropriate and desirable use of its available cash to increase shareholder value.
  • The shares are higher amid Q3 results.
  • DOOO +12.3% premarket to $54.51
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Genius Group acquires XD Academy, bitcoin learning platform
data.description:
Genius Group (NYSE:GNS), an AI-powered bitcoin education group, acquired XD Academy, a bitcoin and blockchain learning platform, in a move that will help Genius build faculty and courses for the Bitcoin Academy on its Edtech platform, the company said Friday.

The company plans to develop the Bitcoin Academy as part of its AI-powered “Built on Bitcoin” decentralized education network, with on-chain GEMs (Genius Education Merits), AI tutors, and AI Certifications built on the Lightning Network, it said.

Genius (NYSE:GNS) stock fell 2.2% in Friday premarket trading.

Terms of the deal weren't disclosed, as the acquisition isn't material in economic terms, the company said.

Genius Group (GNS) will hold an investor meeting from Bitcoin MENA at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: GitLab pops as Wall Street praises results, still sees opportunity
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) was in the spotlight on Friday after the DevOps company reported stronger-than-expected third-quarter results and guidance, leading to praise from Wall Street.

Shares rose 10% in premarket trading, while Microsoft (MSFT), which owns GitLab's competitor GitHub, was up 0.4%.

"We remain encouraged by GitLab's consolidation wins and large deal activity, and are raising our PT to $80 based on 13x FY26E EV/sales accordingly," KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst Jason Celino wrote in a note to clients.

Celino, who has an Overweight rating on GitLab, said the third-quarter results were driven in part by five deals worth $100,000 or more, along with new customer deals incorporating Duo adoption and several new logos and expansions that included Duo Pro and Enterprise.

"We remain encouraged by GitLab's consolidation opportunities in a fragmented DevSecOps market, and view GitLab's Duo SKUs, Premium tier price increases, and GitLab Dedicated positively as catalysts to potential 30%+ growth in FY25 and FY26...," Celino added.

"Favorite growth idea"

Several other firms raised their price targets on GitLab as well, including Raymond James, Barclays and TD Cowen, with many saying there is still room for the stock to move higher.

RBC Capital Markets analysts, led by Matthew Hedberg, were encouraged by the company's "strong results relative to expectations."

"[We] maintain [our Outperform rating] and raise PT to $80 from $73 as we remain impressed by the market opportunity and strong execution," the analysts wrote. "GTLB remains a favorite growth idea."

They also said Bill Staples taking over GitLab as its new CEO is a "good fit" for the company, given his previous work experience. "We feel that he is a good fit for the role with nearly 30 years of experience with building developer platforms and helping scale large enterprises," they wrote. "Overall, we expect a seamless transition and look forward to hearing more about his strategy and vision over the next few quarters."

Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz also praised the company and said he remained confident in the company's "ability to executive and grow at a high level due to its multiple upside levers including seat expansion, price increases, and upsell potential."

Moskowitz reiterated his Outperform rating on GitLab and upped his price target to $80 from $67 after the results.
